title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two students talking about a lost library book.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you found that library book yet? || Man: Not yet. I checked my bag and the classroom where we had maths, but it wasn't there. I was going to buy another copy, but the librarian said they sometimes keep books that students leave behind. [8]So I'm going to ask at the library desk before I do anything else[/8]. || Woman: Good idea. Maybe someone handed it in yesterday. || Woman: Anyway, I’ll let you know what happens, because it is one of those small things that seems easy until you have to deal with it yourself.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you found that library book yet?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not yet. I checked my bag and the classroom where we had maths, but it wasn't there. I was going to buy another copy, but the librarian said they sometimes keep books that students leave behind. So I'm going to ask at the library desk before I do anything else.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Good idea. Maybe someone handed it in yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Anyway, I’ll let you know what happens, because it is one of those small things that seems easy until you have to deal with it yourself.

questions.question: What will the man do next?

options.C: buy a new copy of the book.

questions.explanation: The man says he is going to ask at the library desk before doing anything else.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking about a photography course.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you enjoying your photography course? || Man: Mostly, yes. I thought I'd need a much better camera, but the teacher says mine is fine for now. The homework isn't too bad either; we just take a few pictures each week. [9]The only thing I find hard is getting there for eight o'clock on Saturday mornings[/9]. || Woman: That does sound early for the weekend. || Woman: Still, I’m glad we talked about it, because it helps to hear another opinion before making a final decision about something like this.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you enjoying your photography course?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Mostly, yes. I thought I'd need a much better camera, but the teacher says mine is fine for now. The homework isn't too bad either; we just take a few pictures each week. The only thing I find hard is getting there for eight o'clock on Saturday mornings.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That does sound early for the weekend.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Still, I’m glad we talked about it, because it helps to hear another opinion before making a final decision about something like this.

questions.question: What problem does the man have with the course?

options.A: His camera is not good enough.

questions.explanation: He says the hard part is getting there for eight o'clock on Saturday mornings.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman telling a friend about a concert.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How was the concert last night? || Woman: Better than I expected. Our seats were near the side, so I thought we might not see much, but the view was fine. The sound was a bit loud at first, though it improved later. [10]And the best surprise was the price. We paid half what I thought tickets would cost[/10]. || Man: That's lucky. Concerts are usually expensive these days. || Woman: I suppose it is a good lesson for next time, and at least I know what to check before I make any plans again.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How was the concert last night?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Better than I expected. Our seats were near the side, so I thought we might not see much, but the view was fine. The sound was a bit loud at first, though it improved later. And the best surprise was the price. We paid half what I thought tickets would cost.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's lucky. Concerts are usually expensive these days.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suppose it is a good lesson for next time, and at least I know what to check before I make any plans again.

questions.question: How did the woman feel about the concert tickets?

options.A: She thought they were too expensive.
options.B: She was disappointed with the seats.
options.C: She was pleased with the price.

questions.explanation: She says the best surprise was the price and that the tickets cost half what she expected.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a restaurant.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you like the new restaurant? || Man: Yes. The soup wasn't as hot as I wanted, but the waiter changed it quickly. || Woman: I thought the main courses were fine, though nothing special. [11]For me, the best part was the chocolate cake at the end[/11]. || Man: I agree. I don't usually order dessert, but that was excellent. We should go back just for that. || Woman: Anyway, it was useful to talk it through, because now the whole situation feels a bit clearer than it did before.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you like the new restaurant?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. The soup wasn't as hot as I wanted, but the waiter changed it quickly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I thought the main courses were fine, though nothing special. For me, the best part was the chocolate cake at the end.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. I don't usually order dessert, but that was excellent. We should go back just for that.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Anyway, it was useful to talk it through, because now the whole situation feels a bit clearer than it did before.

questions.question: They both think that

options.A: the dessert was the best part.
options.B: the soup was too cold to eat.

questions.explanation: The woman says the chocolate cake was the best part, and the man agrees it was excellent.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking to a colleague about moving house.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did your move go smoothly at the weekend? || Woman: Not really. We collected the keys on time, and the traffic wasn't bad, which was a relief. [12]But the van we hired broke down before it even reached our old flat[/12]. We had to wait two hours for another one. || Man: Oh no. That must have made the day very long. || Woman: I’ll remember that for the future, because small details like that can make a normal day much easier or much more difficult.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did your move go smoothly at the weekend?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not really. We collected the keys on time, and the traffic wasn't bad, which was a relief. But the van we hired broke down before it even reached our old flat. We had to wait two hours for another one.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Oh no. That must have made the day very long.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ll remember that for the future, because small details like that can make a normal day much easier or much more difficult.

questions.question: Why was the woman's move delayed?
questions.explanation: She says the van they hired broke down before it reached their old flat.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about buying a phone.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you still thinking of repairing your old phone? || Man: I was, but the shop said it would cost almost as much as replacing it. I looked at the newest model, but I don't need such a big screen or camera. [13]In the end, I've chosen last year's model because it has everything I use and it's much cheaper[/13]. || Woman: That sounds sensible. || Woman: That’s true. I think I know what to do now, and I’m glad I didn’t make the decision too quickly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you still thinking of repairing your old phone?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I was, but the shop said it would cost almost as much as replacing it. I looked at the newest model, but I don't need such a big screen or camera. In the end, I've chosen last year's model because it has everything I use and it's much cheaper.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds sensible.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That’s true. I think I know what to do now, and I’m glad I didn’t make the decision too quickly.

questions.question: What has the man decided to buy?

options.B: parts to repair his old phone.

questions.explanation: The man says he has chosen last year's model because it is cheaper and has what he needs.
